data.title: Occidental Petroleum Earnings Are Imminent; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
data.description:
Occidental Petroleum Corporation (NYSE:OXY) will release earnings results for its fourth quarter, after the closing bell on Wednesday, Feb. 18.

Analysts expect the Houston, Texas-based company to report quarterly earnings at 18 cents per share, down from 80 cents per share in the year-ago period. The consensus estimate for Occidental Petroleum's quarterly revenue is $5.62 billion, versus $6.84 billion a year earlier, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

On Jan. 2, Occidental announced that it has completed the sale of its chemical business, OxyChem, to Berkshire Hathaway.

Occidental Petroleum shares fell 0.3% to close at $45.94 on Tuesday.

Benzinga readers can access the latest analyst ratings on the Analyst Stock Ratings page. Readers can sort by stock ticker, company name, analyst firm, rating change or other variables.

Let's have a look at how Benzinga's most-accurate analysts have rated the company in the recent period.

data.title: How To Earn $500 A Month From eBay Stock Ahead Of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
eBay Inc. (NASDAQ:EBAY) will release earnings for its fourth quarter after the closing bell on Wednesday, Feb. 18.

Analysts expect the company to report quarterly earnings of $1.35 per share. That’s up from $1.25 per share in the year-ago period. The consensus estimate for eBay's quarterly revenue is $2.87 billion (it reported $2.58 billion last year), according to Benzinga Pro.

Ahead of quarterly earnings, UBS analyst Stephen Ju, on Tuesday, maintained a Neutral rating on eBay and lowered the price target from $95 to $93.

With the recent buzz around eBay, some investors may be eyeing potential gains from the company's dividends too. As of now, eBay has an annual dividend yield of 1.45%, which is a quarterly dividend amount of 29 cents per share ($1.16 a year).

So, how can investors exploit its dividend yield to pocket a regular $500 monthly?

To earn $500 per month or $6,000 annually from dividends alone, you would need an investment of approximately $413,501 or around 5,172 shares. For a more modest $100 per month or $1,200 per year, you would need $82,668 or around 1,034 shares.

To calculate: Divide the desired annual income ($6,000 or $1,200) by the dividend ($1.16 in this case). So, $6,000 / $1.16 = 5,172 ($500 per month), and $1,200 / $1.16 = 1,034 shares ($100 per month).

Note that dividend yield can change on a rolling basis, as the dividend payment and the stock price both fluctuate over time.

How that works: The dividend yield is computed by dividing the annual dividend payment by the stock’s current price.

For example, if a stock pays an annual dividend of $2 and is currently priced at $50, the dividend yield would be 4% ($2/$50). However, if the stock price increases to $60, the dividend yield drops to 3.33% ($2/$60). Conversely, if the stock price falls to $40, the dividend yield rises to 5% ($2/$40).

Similarly, changes in the dividend payment can impact the yield. If a company increases its dividend, the yield will also increase, provided the stock price stays the same. Conversely, if the dividend payment decreases, so will the yield.

EBAY Price Action: Shares of eBay fell by 3% to close at $79.95 on Tuesday.

Photo: JHVEPhoto via Shutterstock

data.title: Wix, Intuit Expand Partnership To Power Small Business Growth
data.description:
Wix.com Ltd. (NASDAQ:WIX) and Intuit Inc. (NASDAQ:INTU) announced on Tuesday an expanded partnership aimed at delivering a more integrated experience for small businesses managing both their digital presence and financial operations.

The integration brings Intuit QuickBooks and Wix users a unified way to streamline their business sales, finances, and operations alongside their web and e-commerce sites.

Integrated Financial And Website Management Tools

Wix users can now access QuickBooks directly on the Intuit platform, enabling businesses to run and scale more efficiently. Through the integration of QuickBooks Online, millions of Wix users gain deeper visibility into profitability, cash flow, and estimated tax liabilities.

The partnership also introduces automated accounting capabilities powered by QuickBooks’ AI-driven tools, helping businesses manage finances, optimize online sales, and reduce administrative workload.

In addition, Wix users can connect their CRM data with Intuit Mailchimp, strengthening marketing efforts to drive customer engagement and revenue growth.

Benefits For QuickBooks Customers

The collaboration also extends benefits to QuickBooks customers. QuickBooks enables users to launch tailored Wix websites with existing business information pre-populated, simplifying the transition to an online presence.

Through Intuit’s platform, QuickBooks customers can seamlessly build and manage AI-powered, contextually aware Wix websites within their existing workflow. Users gain access to Wix’s site-building tools, AI agents, built-in SEO features, and mobile optimization capabilities, allowing businesses to streamline daily operations while maintaining their financial management foundation.

Executive Commentary

Price Action: Wix.com shares were up 1.43% at $69.04 during premarket trading on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data. Intuit shares were up 0.18% at $379.85.

Photo by Teo K via Shutterstock

data.title: Why Is Palo Alto Networks Stock Falling Today?
data.description:
The cybersecurity firm’s mixed guidance reaction appeared to overshadow management’s optimism around recent acquisitions and growing AI-driven demand.

On Tuesday, the cybersecurity company lowered its full-year adjusted earnings guidance from a range of $3.80 to $3.90 per share to a range of $3.65 to $3.70 per share, versus estimates of $3.86 per share.

The company reported fiscal second-quarter revenue of $2.59 billion, beating analyst estimates of $2.58 billion. The cybersecurity company posted adjusted earnings of $1.03 per share for the quarter, beating analyst estimates of 94 cents per share.

Total revenue was up 15% year-over-year and remaining performance obligations grew 23% year-over-year to $16 billion. The company said next-generation security annual recurring revenue increased 33% year-over-year to $6.3 billion.

Palo Alto ended the quarter with approximately $4.16 billion in cash and cash equivalents.

Markets ‘Have It Wrong’

However, in an interview with the Bloomberg Television, Arora said, “I think the market is not paying attention to our numbers carefully.”

Regarding the guidance cut, Arora explained that the guidance for the next two quarters includes CyberArk.

In its earnings call, the CEO said that they were excited to head into the second half of the year after closing the CyberArk and Chronosphere acquisitions, and added that they wanted to extend a warm welcome to both teams.

“If you take the consensus of CyberArk and ours, you’re actually guiding above the collective consensus,” Arora said. He highlighted that the market has not understood the dilution of shares, adding, “So I think they have it wrong”.

Notably, the firm reported fiscal second-quarter revenue of $2.59 billion, beating analyst estimates of $2.58 billion.

During its earnings call, the CEO added that, after closing the Chronosphere acquisition, the company signed a multi-year, nine-figure expansion deal with a leading AI model provider in the second quarter.

Palo Alto also raised its fiscal 2026 revenue guidance.

The company now expects full-year revenue of $11.28 billion to $11.31 billion, up from prior guidance of $10.50 billion to $10.54 billion. Analysts are expecting full-year revenue of $10.55 billion.

Analyst’s Rating

BTIG analyst Gray Powell reiterated a Buy rating on the stock, maintaining $200 price forecast.

Needham analyst Mike Cikos maintained Palo Alto Networks with a Buy, lowering the price forecast from $230 to $200.

PANW Price Action: Palo Alto Networks shares were down 7.22% at $151.69 during premarket trading on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Nvidia Partner Super Micro's Momentum Drops As Investors Shrug Off Earnings Surprise— Shares Approach 52-Week Low
data.description:
Fundamental Strength Clashes With Market Sentiment

Despite reporting a massive second-quarter earnings beat, the stock's momentum score has collapsed from 16.26 to 6.35 week-on-week, according to the latest Benzinga Edge Stock Rankings.

This drop places the AI-server giant in the bottom 10% for relative strength, as investors appear to prioritize technical weakness over operational success.

The company posted adjusted earnings of $0.69 per share on revenue of $12.68 billion, easily topping consensus estimates of $0.49 and $10.21 billion, respectively.

While management raised full-year revenue guidance to $40 billion, the market’s reaction has been muted by ongoing volatility and analyst price target reductions from firms like Needham and Rosenblatt.

Benzinga Edge Rankings Reveal Deep Technical Erosion

While Super Micro maintains elite fundamental scores, including a quality rank of 98.52 and a growth rank of 82.57, its price-driven metrics have turned sharply negative.

The price trends for short, medium, and long terms are all currently flashing red.

SMCI is trading 34.11% below its 200-day simple moving average (SMA) of $41.69, as per Benzinga Pro, reflecting a mixed to bearish technical picture that has seen shares decrease by 46.04% over the past year.

Investor Outlook Amidst Momentum Decay

The combination of a neutral Relative Strength Index of 45.40 and a momentum score nearing zero suggests significant uncertainty in the stock's short-term trajectory.

Although the value rank of 80.49 indicates the stock may be underpriced relative to its assets and earnings, the weak momentum score serves as a warning for short-term traders.

SMCI Underperforms In 2026

Shares of SMCI have declined by 2.75% year-to-date, while the Nasdaq Composite was down 2.83% in the same period.

The stock was down 2.83% over the last six months and 46.04% over the year. On Wednesday, the stock was 1.63% higher in premarket trading.
